evidence.text: More than 97% of our revenue has historically been derived from transporting LTL shipments for our customers, whose demand for our services is generally tied to industrial production and the overall health of the U.S. domestic economy.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: The summary indicates accelerating demand, which is a positive trend.

factors.summary: The company is successfully raising prices.

evidence.text: LTL revenue per hundredweight increased 4.9% to $19.03 in the first quarter of 2017 as compared to the same period of 2016, despite the downward pressure on this metric created by an increase in weight per shipment and a decrease in average length of haul.

sentiment.label: very_positive
sentiment.rationale: The company successfully raised prices despite challenges, showing strong pricing power.

evidence.text: Our revenue increased $46.4 million in the first quarter of 2017, or 6.6% as compared to the first quarter of 2016, due to an increase of $45.7 million in revenue from our LTL services and a $0.7 million increase in our non-LTL service revenue.

sentiment.label: very_positive
sentiment.rationale: Revenue growth accelerated, indicating strong organic expansion.

evidence.text: LTL tons per day increased 4.3% in April 2017 primarily due to a 4.2% increase in LTL shipments per day, while LTL weight per shipment remained consistent as compared to April 2016.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Unit volumes are growing, reflecting increased demand.

factors.summary: Input costs are rising, particularly diesel fuel costs.

evidence.text: Operating supplies and expenses increased $15.6 million in the first quarter of 2017 due primarily to the increase in diesel fuel costs as compared to the first quarter of 2016.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Input costs, particularly diesel fuel, are rising, which could pressure margins.

factors.summary: Labor costs are manageable with productivity improvements.

evidence.text: Our aggregate productive labor costs improved to 28.7% of revenue in the first quarter of 2017 from 29.6% in the first quarter of 2016. Productivity improvements allowed us to efficiently manage the additional freight volumes.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Labor costs are manageable due to productivity improvements, which is a positive development.

factors.summary: Gross and operating margins are expanding.

evidence.text: Our operating ratio improved by 20 basis points to 85.7% in the first quarter of 2017 from 85.9% in the first quarter of 2016, contributing to higher net income.

sentiment.label: very_positive
sentiment.rationale: Gross and operating margins are expanding, indicating strong profitability trends.

evidence.text: Productivity improvements allowed us to efficiently manage the additional freight volumes. Our platform pounds per hour and P&D shipments per hour each improved 1.6%, and our P&D stops per hour increased 0.7%, as compared to the first quarter of 2016.

sentiment.label: very_positive
sentiment.rationale: Operational efficiency is improving across multiple metrics, which is a strong positive signal.

factors.summary: Management is confident in future growth.

evidence.text: We expect to continue to maintain a high level of capital expenditures in order to support our long-term plan for market share growth.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Management's confidence in future growth is supported by their investment in capital expenditures.

factors.summary: The company has a clear long-term strategic direction.

evidence.text: We expect to continue to maintain a high level of capital expenditures in order to support our long-term plan for market share growth.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: The company has a clear long-term strategic direction with a focus on market share growth.

factors.summary: Diesel fuel prices and economic conditions are key sensitivities.

evidence.text: Our fuel surcharges are designed to offset fluctuations in the cost of petroleum-based products and are one of the many components included in the overall negotiated price we charge for our services.

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: The company is aware of key sensitivities but has measures in place to manage them.

factors.summary: The company is increasing capital expenditures.

evidence.text: We currently estimate capital expenditures will be approximately $385 million for the year ending December 31, 2017.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: The company is increasing capital expenditures, indicating a growth-oriented strategy.

evidence.text: As of March 31, 2017, we were in compliance with our financial covenants, and we do not anticipate a significant decline in business levels or financial performance that would cause us to violate any such covenants in the future.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: The balance sheet is strengthening, with compliance and no anticipated decline in financial performance.

factors.summary: The company is returning capital to shareholders through dividends.

evidence.text: On February 2, 2017, we announced a quarterly cash dividend of $0.10 per share, which was paid on March 20, 2017.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: The company is returning capital to shareholders through dividends.

factors.summary: Free cash flow generation is improving.

evidence.text: Cash flows provided by operating activities increased to $110.8 million in the first quarter of 2017 from $168.4 million in the first quarter of 2016.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Cash flows provided by operating activities decreased compared to the previous year.
